Active Work Architecture
─[ Field Fix Metadata Format ]─ ┐

Field Fix Metadata Format

Define the structured metadata schema that serves as the contract between instances and the source triage pipeline. Every field fix submission carries this metadata — the intake workflow parses it, the AI review gate evaluates against it, and the grouping layer uses it for similarity matching.

Goal

A single, parseable metadata format that captures everything the source repo needs to evaluate a field fix without access to the originating instance.

Schema Fields

Required

  • Bug description — What broke, with reproduction steps
  • Root cause — Why it broke (the insight that led to the fix)
  • Diff — The actual fix, keyed to the Lore version it applies against
  • Lore version — Exact version tag (e.g. v0.13.0)
  • Affected component — Category: hooks, scripts, lib, skills, config, docs
  • Platform — OS and tool where the bug was discovered (e.g. linux/claude-code, macos/cursor)
  • Scope declaration — List of files modified, confirming all are harness-owned
  • Security self-check — Explicit attestation: no instance-specific data, no secrets, no local paths

Recommended

  • Test evidence — How the fix was verified (test output, before/after behavior)
  • Cross-platform assessment — Does this fix affect other platforms? Has it been tested on them?
  • Convention compliance — Did validate-consistency.sh pass after the fix?
  • Node version — Runtime version where the fix was developed/tested
  • Related issues — Links to existing GitHub issues if known

Optional

  • Alternative approaches considered — What else was tried and why this approach won
  • Regression risk — What could break if this fix is applied broadly

Deliverables

  • Issue template (.github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/field-fix.yml) in lore source repo — structured form with required/optional fields mapping to the schema above
  • PR template section or dedicated PR template for field-fix PRs — parseable by GitHub Actions
  • Schema reference doc — canonical spec referenced by the submit skill and triage workflow

Format Decisions

  • YAML frontmatter in PR body — Machine-parseable section at the top of the PR description, followed by human-readable narrative. GitHub Actions can extract the YAML block reliably.
  • Issue template uses YAML form — GitHub’s issue form syntax (.yml) provides structured fields with validation, dropdowns for component/platform, and required field enforcement.

Scope

Target repo: /home/andrew/Github/lore (source framework)

Files to create/modify:

  • .github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/field-fix.yml — new
  • .github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE/field-fix.md — new (or section in existing template)
  • docs/knowledge/reference/field-fix-schema.md — new (canonical schema doc, travels with instances via sync)

Success Criteria

  • Issue template renders correctly on GitHub and enforces required fields
  • PR template YAML block is parseable by a simple Node.js script
  • Schema doc is clear enough for an AI agent to generate compliant metadata without examples
  • Security self-check field cannot be left empty or skipped
  • validate-consistency.sh passes in lore source after adding templates

Test Plan

# In lore source repo
bash scripts/validate-consistency.sh

# Validate issue template YAML is valid
node -e "const yaml = require('yaml'); const fs = require('fs'); yaml.parse(fs.readFileSync('.github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/field-fix.yml', 'utf8')); console.log('valid')"
PgUp/PgDn: scroll b: bulk commands q: quit x: menu ← → ↑ ↓: navigate
